census_bps_permits.source: Census Building Permits Survey via FRED (county sum over 13027,13101,13173,13185; SF/MF split estimated from GA state 1-unit share (county 1-unit unavailable))

ita_msa_exports.source: ITA Metropolitan Area Exports (annual CSV refresh — maed_2024.csv)

epa_air_quality.source: EPA AirData annual AQI by CBSA, 2025 (https://aqs.epa.gov/aqsweb/airdata/annual_aqi_by_cbsa_2025.zip)

acs_affordability.source: Census ACS 5-year B25064 (median gross rent) / B19013 (median HH income), MSA vs US

census_net_migration.source: Census PEP components of change (CO-EST2025), MSA counties

acs_housing_characteristics.source: Census ACS 5-year, 2024 vintage (B25024 structure, B25035 year built) + Census Gazetteer land area

acs_block_group_income.source: Census ACS 5-year 2024 block-group median HH income (B19013), MSA counties

entrepreneurship.source: Census Business Dynamics Statistics (establishment entry rate, 2022); metro rate indexed to US=100

major_employers.method: Representative list auto-discovered via Tavily web search across chamber-of-commerce, development-authority and local-news sources. Not exhaustive; ordering approximates source frequency, not headcount.

business_cycle_index.method: Stock-Watson coincident (2-input PCA, first principal component (positive co-movement, equal-weight loadings))
business_cycle_index.source: EIG composite — computed from BLS CES + LAUS

forecast_arima.method: Damped-trend Holt smoothing (ARIMA(1,1,2) family), per-series grid-fit on annual actuals; level series modeled in log space
forecast_arima.source: EIG forecast composite — computed from BLS/BEA/Census/FHFA actuals

vitality.method: EIG composite: weighted average of z-scores for LFP, income growth, young-adult share and net-migration rate vs. approximate U.S.-metro benchmarks; score = normal CDF of the mean z.
vitality.note: Rank is an estimate from the composite's normal percentile against reference benchmarks, not a live ranking of all metros.
vitality.source: EIG composite — computed from ACS, BEA and IRS SOI inputs

quality_of_life.method: EIG composite: weighted average of sign-adjusted z-scores for air quality, commute, affordability, poverty and school spending vs. approximate U.S.-metro benchmarks; scaled to 0-300 (150 = average).
quality_of_life.note: Crime excluded — no clean automated current MSA-level source exists (FBI API is agency/state only; the FBI by-MSA table ended in 2019). Rank is an estimate from the composite's normal percentile.
quality_of_life.source: EIG composite — computed from EPA, ACS and Census F-33 inputs

housing_valuation.method: Log-log fair-value regression (Dallas Fed / IMF house-price-gap method): log(FHFA HPI) on log(per-capita income) by OLS over overlapping years; valuation = % deviation of actual HPI from the income-implied fair value.
housing_valuation.note: Fitted on 6 overlapping years (2019-2024); residuals are mean-zero over this window, so values read relative to the local income trend. Price-to-income / price-to-rent are current ACS-vintage cross-checks, not part of the fitted series.
housing_valuation.source: EIG valuation model — FHFA HPI vs. BEA per-capita income (log-log OLS)

business_costs.method: EIG composite (BEA Regional-Price-Parity logic): cost of living = expenditure-weighted blend of rents (0.36), tradable goods (0.15, ~national) and wage-elastic services (0.49); cost of doing business = labor (0.62, wage proxy), occupancy (0.20, rent) and tax/other (0.18, ~national). US = 100.
business_costs.note: Wage proxy is per-capita income (QCEW average annual wage will replace it once that section populates). US benchmarks are ACS 5-year national approximations: median gross rent $1,400, per-capita income $43,000.
business_costs.source: EIG composite — computed from ACS rent + income vs. national benchmarks

industrial_diversity.method: Hachman index = 1 / Σ(local_share² / US_share) over QCEW super-sectors; 1.0 = industrial mix identical to the U.S. (most balanced/diversified), lower = more concentrated.
industrial_diversity.source: EIG composite — computed from QCEW employment shares (MSA & GA vs. US)

housing_affordability.source: EIG composite — NAR-style affordability: ACS median household income (B19013) vs. income needed for the median home (ACS B25077 value scaled by FHFA HPI; Freddie Mac PMMS 30-yr rate via FRED). >100 = median household can afford the median home.

credit_score.method: EIG composite credit rating: weighted mean of normalized sub-scores for vitality (0.25), business-cycle position (0.20), labor market (0.20), quality of life (0.15), income growth (0.10) and valuation stability (0.10); scaled 0-100 and mapped to a muni-bond-style letter grade. Outlook from business-cycle momentum and income growth.
credit_score.note: A relative creditworthiness signal for the metro economy, not a household FICO score and not a rated municipal obligation. Sub-scores are percentile positions vs. approximate US-metro reference points.
credit_score.source: EIG composite — synthesized from vitality, BCI, QoL, BLS, BEA and valuation
